# MITRE ATT&CK Mapping: Edge-Device KEV CVEs (Heuristic, Operational)

**Dataset:** 115 CISA KEV (Known Exploited Vulnerabilities) CVEs across 13 edge-device vendors (Cisco, Citrix, F5, Fortinet, Ivanti, Juniper, Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall, Sophos, Check Point, Zyxel, WatchGuard, Array Networks)

**Generated by:** `scripts/enrich_attack.py` (writes `attack_techniques`, `pre_auth`, `eol_at_kev_date` into `scripts/kev_edge_enriched.json`; exported to `scripts/edge_kev_dataset.csv`)

> **READ THIS FIRST — this is a heuristic, not ground truth.** The technique
> IDs below are derived by a *deterministic, documented* rule from each CVE's
> NVD-assigned CWE plus its exploitation context (internet-facing edge
> appliance; SSL-VPN / remote-access product family). They are **NOT
> authoritative MITRE ATT&CK assignments.** CISA does not publish per-CVE
> ATT&CK techniques for most KEV entries, and the CWE→technique relationship
> is many-to-many. Treat every technique here as a **detection/hunting
> hypothesis to validate against the actual exploit**, not as a fact about how
> the CVE was used in the wild. Threat-actor-attributed techniques (where a
> named campaign is known) live in `docs/THREAT-ACTORS.md` and
> `docs/THREAT-ATTRIBUTION.md`; this file is the *structural* mapping.

---

## Why this exists

The KEV catalog is the authoritative list of *exploited* vulnerabilities, but
it ships almost no ATT&CK context. Per CISA/runZero analysis of the catalog
([RELATED-WORK.md](./RELATED-WORK.md), Beardsley/KEVology), only roughly **424
of ~1,488** KEV entries carried any ATT&CK technique mapping — meaning the vast
majority of exploited CVEs give a SOC no ATT&CK starting point at all. (That
ratio is a published, approximate figure from third-party analysis of an
earlier catalog snapshot, cited here to motivate the gap; it is not a count we
computed.) This file fills that gap for the 115 edge-device CVEs in scope, with
an explicit, reproducible methodology so the result is auditable rather than
hand-waved.

---

## (a) Technique catalogue

| Technique | Name | Tactic | When assigned |
|-----------|------|--------|---------------|
| **T1190** | Exploit Public-Facing Application | Initial Access | **Every** in-scope CVE — the vulnerable surface is an internet-facing appliance. |
| **T1133** | External Remote Services | Initial Access / Persistence | CVE affects an SSL-VPN / remote-access product family (Ivanti Connect Secure, Citrix Gateway, Array AG, or any product whose KEV description names an SSL-VPN / Secure Mobile Access / remote-access surface). |
| **T1078** | Valid Accounts | Initial Access / Persistence | Auth-bypass / missing-auth / improper-authorization CWE that yields a usable account or session. |
| **T1212** | Exploitation for Credential Access | Credential Access | Information-exposure / session weaknesses (CWE-200, CWE-613) — CitrixBleed-class. |
| **T1552** | Unsecured Credentials | Credential Access | Path-traversal / file-disclosure / credential-storage CWEs that leak credentials or config (CWE-22, CWE-200, CWE-522, CWE-798, …). |
| **T1068** | Exploitation for Privilege Escalation | Privilege Escalation | Privilege-management / permission CWEs (CWE-269, CWE-732, CWE-250, …). |
| **T1505.003** | Server Software Component: Web Shell | Persistence | Unrestricted file upload (CWE-434) on the appliance web component. |

---

## (b) Deterministic mapping rule

The mapping is implemented as an explicit `CWE_TECHNIQUE_TABLE` in
`scripts/enrich_attack.py`. The algorithm for each CVE is:

1. **Base technique.** Assign **T1190** unconditionally — every CVE in this
   dataset is, by the dataset's own scope rule, a network-reachable flaw in an
   internet-facing edge appliance.
2. **CWE-driven secondary techniques.** Look up the CVE's NVD *primary* CWE in
   the table and add the listed technique(s). (Only the NVD primary CWE is
   used; secondary CWEs are not, to keep the rule single-valued and
   reproducible.)
3. **Product-driven T1133.** If the vendor is a pure remote-access vendor
   (Ivanti, Citrix, Array Networks) **or** the KEV `shortDescription` names an
   SSL-VPN / Secure Mobile Access / remote-access surface, add **T1133**. This
   is independent of the CWE — it reflects *what service* is exposed, not *what
   bug class*.
4. **De-duplicate**, preserving insertion order (T1190 always first).

### CWE → technique table (CWEs actually present in this dataset)

The full table in the script covers ~60 CWEs; below are only the CWEs that
appear in these 115 CVEs, with the **CWE-driven** techniques (T1133 is *not*
shown here because it is product-driven, see step 3).

| CWE | Name | CWE-driven techniques |
|-----|------|-----------------------|
| CWE-20 | Improper Input Validation | T1190 |
| CWE-22 | Path Traversal | T1190, T1552 |
| CWE-73 | External Control of File Name/Path | T1190, T1552 |
| CWE-77 | Command Injection | T1190 |
| CWE-78 | OS Command Injection | T1190 |
| CWE-79 | Cross-Site Scripting | T1190 |
| CWE-89 | SQL Injection | T1190 |
| CWE-94 | Code Injection | T1190 |
| CWE-119 | Improper Restriction of Memory Buffer | T1190 |
| CWE-120 | Classic Buffer Overflow | T1190 |
| CWE-121 | Stack-based Buffer Overflow | T1190 |
| CWE-122 | Heap-based Buffer Overflow | T1190 |
| CWE-125 | Out-of-bounds Read | T1190 |
| CWE-134 | Format String | T1190 |
| CWE-197 | Numeric Truncation Error | T1190 |
| CWE-200 | Exposure of Sensitive Information | T1190, T1212, T1552 |
| CWE-269 | Improper Privilege Management | T1068 |
| CWE-284 | Improper Access Control | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-287 | Improper Authentication | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-288 | Auth Bypass via Alternate Path/Channel | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-306 | Missing Authentication for Critical Function | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-347 | Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature | T1190 |
| CWE-406 | Insufficient Control of Network Message Volume | T1190 |
| CWE-434 | Unrestricted File Upload | T1190, T1505.003 |
| CWE-473 | PHP External Variable Modification | T1190 |
| CWE-494 | Download of Code Without Integrity Check | T1190 |
| CWE-502 | Deserialization of Untrusted Data | T1190 |
| CWE-522 | Insufficiently Protected Credentials | T1190, T1552 |
| CWE-565 | Reliance on Cookies without Validation | T1190 |
| CWE-653 | Improper Isolation/Compartmentalization | T1190 |
| CWE-664 | Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime | T1190 |
| CWE-732 | Incorrect Permission Assignment | T1068 |
| CWE-754 | Improper Check for Unusual Conditions | T1190 |
| CWE-772 | Missing Release of Resource | T1190 |
| CWE-787 | Out-of-bounds Write | T1190 |
| CWE-798 | Use of Hard-coded Credentials | T1190, T1552 |
| CWE-835 | Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition | T1190 |
| CWE-862 | Missing Authorization | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-863 | Incorrect Authorization | T1190, T1078 |
| CWE-918 | Server-Side Request Forgery | T1190 |

The 2 CVEs with no NVD CWE assignment receive only the base **T1190** (plus
**T1133** if they are on a remote-access product).

---

## (c) Per-technique CVE rollup

CVE counts per technique across all 115 CVEs:

| Technique | Name | CVEs | Share |
|-----------|------|-----:|------:|
| T1190 | Exploit Public-Facing Application | 115 | 100% |
| T1133 | External Remote Services | 51 | 44% |
| T1078 | Valid Accounts | 25 | 22% |
| T1552 | Unsecured Credentials | 13 | 11% |
| T1212 | Exploitation for Credential Access | 2 | 2% |
| T1068 | Exploitation for Privilege Escalation | 2 | 2% |
| T1505.003 | Web Shell | 1 | 1% |

**Interpretation:** Every edge CVE is, structurally, T1190 — that is the
defining property of the dataset, not a finding. The operationally useful
signal is the **44% T1133 share**: nearly half of these CVEs put the
*remote-access service itself* on the attack surface, which is exactly the
class of access that bypasses perimeter assumptions. The **22% T1078** share
reflects how many edge bugs are authentication/authorization failures that hand
an attacker a valid session rather than a crash.

### T1078 — Valid Accounts (25 CVEs)

CVE-2015-7755 (Juniper), CVE-2018-13382 / CVE-2019-5591 / CVE-2020-12812 /
CVE-2022-40684 / CVE-2024-55591 / CVE-2025-24472 / CVE-2026-24858 (Fortinet),
CVE-2020-8193 / CVE-2020-8196 (Citrix), CVE-2022-1388 / CVE-2023-46747 (F5),
CVE-2023-20269 / CVE-2025-20362 (Cisco), CVE-2021-22893 (Ivanti),
CVE-2023-36846 / CVE-2023-36847 / CVE-2023-36851 (Juniper), CVE-2024-0012 /
CVE-2025-0108 (Palo Alto Networks), CVE-2024-40766 / CVE-2024-53704 /
CVE-2025-40602 (SonicWall), CVE-2023-28461 (Array Networks), CVE-2026-50751
(Check Point).

### T1552 — Unsecured Credentials (13 CVEs)

CVE-2018-13379 / CVE-2019-6693 / CVE-2022-41328 (Fortinet), CVE-2019-19781
(Citrix), CVE-2020-5902 (F5), CVE-2019-11510 (Ivanti), CVE-2020-1631
(Juniper), CVE-2025-0111 (Palo Alto Networks), CVE-2019-7483 (SonicWall),
CVE-2020-29583 / CVE-2024-11667 (Zyxel), CVE-2024-24919 (Check Point),
CVE-2020-3259 (Cisco).

### T1212 — Exploitation for Credential Access (2 CVEs)

CVE-2024-24919 (Check Point), CVE-2020-3259 (Cisco). *(CWE-200 information
exposure.)*

### T1068 — Exploitation for Privilege Escalation (2 CVEs)

CVE-2018-13374 (Fortinet, CWE-732), CVE-2022-23176 (WatchGuard, CWE-269).

### T1505.003 — Web Shell (1 CVE)

CVE-2020-8260 (Ivanti, CWE-434 unrestricted file upload).

> **Note on CitrixBleed (CVE-2023-4966):** the canonical session-token-theft
> case. NVD's *primary* CWE for it is CWE-119, so under the single-CWE rule it
> maps to T1190 + T1133, **not** T1212/T1552. This is a deliberate consequence
> of using the NVD primary CWE for reproducibility — it illustrates exactly why
> this mapping is a hypothesis layer, not ground truth. Analysts hunting
> credential theft on NetScaler should still treat CVE-2023-4966 as
> T1212/T1552 based on the known exploit behaviour.

---

## (d) `pre_auth` field

**Definition:** inferred boolean — `true` only where the CVE is exploitable
*before authentication*. Computed **conservatively**:

- `true` if the NVD primary CWE is an unambiguous missing/weak/bypassable
  authentication class: **CWE-287, CWE-288, CWE-290, CWE-306, CWE-1390**; **or**
- `true` if the CISA KEV `shortDescription` matches an explicit pre-auth phrase
  (`unauthenticated`, `without authentication`, `pre-auth`, `authentication
  bypass`, `does not require authentication`, `allows a remote unauthenticated …`, etc.); **otherwise**
- `false`.

This is **inferred from CWE + KEV description text**, not asserted by the
vendor. A `false` does **not** prove a CVE requires authentication — it means
the conservative signals did not fire (e.g. authorization-only flaws, or
descriptions that omit the auth precondition). When in doubt the field errs
toward `false`.

**Result:** **57 / 115 (50%)** pre-auth. Per-vendor:

| Vendor | pre-auth / total |
|--------|-----------------:|
| Fortinet | 13/18 |
| Juniper | 7/8 |
| Palo Alto Networks | 7/12 |
| Ivanti | 6/13 |
| SonicWall | 6/12 |
| Citrix | 4/13 |
| WatchGuard | 3/4 |
| Zyxel | 3/6 |
| Cisco | 2/13 |
| F5 | 2/6 |
| Sophos | 2/6 |
| Array Networks | 1/2 |
| Check Point | 1/2 |

---

## (e) `eol_at_kev_date` field

**Definition:** boolean — `true` only for the hand-verified cases where the
affected product was already **end-of-life / end-of-support** at the time CISA
added the CVE to KEV. This set is **not inferred**; it is an explicit allow-list
in the script, defaulting to `false` for everything else.

| CVE | Vendor | Product (EOL context) |
|-----|--------|-----------------------|
| CVE-2015-7755 | Juniper | ScreenOS (legacy NetScreen line; the Dual_EC-era hardcoded-credentials backdoor) |
| CVE-2020-29574 | Sophos | CyberoamOS / CROS (Cyberoam product line sunset post-acquisition) |
| CVE-2020-15069 | Sophos | Sophos XG Firewall v17.x firmware branch (end-of-life branch) |

These 3 entries are flagged because "patch it" is not actionable advice when no
supported firmware exists — the SOC action is *replace/segment*, not *update*.
All three CVEs are verified present in the dataset by the script before
flagging; if any were missing the run aborts.

---

## Methodology & limitations (read before using)

- **Heuristic, not authoritative.** This mapping is a structural inference from
  CWE + product context. It is not a MITRE-published or CISA-published
  per-CVE technique assignment, and it does not encode observed
  in-the-wild TTPs (those are in the threat-actor docs).
- **Single primary CWE.** Only the NVD *primary* CWE drives the CWE-based
  techniques, for reproducibility. CVEs whose real-world exploitation hinges on
  a secondary behaviour (e.g. CitrixBleed) can be under-mapped — see the note
  above.
- **T1190 is definitional.** A 100% T1190 share is a property of the dataset's
  scope (internet-facing edge appliances), not an analytic result.
- **pre_auth is conservative and can undercount.** Authorization-only bypasses
  and CVEs with terse KEV descriptions may be genuinely pre-auth yet flagged
  `false`. The field is a floor, not a census.
- **eol_at_kev_date is a curated allow-list of 3.** It is not a systematic EOL
  audit of all 115 CVEs; other CVEs *may* have affected EOL configurations that
  are not flagged here.
- **Reproducible.** Re-run `python3 scripts/enrich_attack.py --kev-file <kev.json>`
  (or with no `--kev-file` to auto-fetch the live CISA feed) against the same
  `kev_edge_enriched.json` and KEV catalog to regenerate identical fields. The
  KEV catalog version used is recorded in `_metadata.attack_kev_catalog_version`.

### References

- MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise techniques: <https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/enterprise/>
  (T1190, T1133, T1078, T1212, T1552, T1068, T1505.003)
- CISA KEV catalog: <https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog>
- Catalog ATT&CK-coverage gap discussion: [RELATED-WORK.md](./RELATED-WORK.md)
- CWE weakness-class breakdown: [CWE-ANALYSIS.md](./CWE-ANALYSIS.md)
- Field definitions: [DATA-DICTIONARY.md](./DATA-DICTIONARY.md)
